stepAnswer: The denominator is zero only when $$\tan(\frac{x}{2})$$ is undefined.
stepTitle: Which of the following best explains why dividing by $$0$$ is not a concern for the half-angle formula for $$\tan(\frac{x}{2})$$?
answerLatex: The denominator is zero only when $$\tan(\frac{x}{2})$$ is undefined.
choices: The denominator is zero only when $$\tan(\frac{x}{2})$$ is undefined.
The denominator is zero only when $$\sin$$ $$x$$ $$=$$ $$0$$.
The denominator is zero only when $$\cos$$ $$x$$ $$=$$ $$1$$.
